A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE TEAM HAS SURVEYED THE DAMAGE IN ST. BERNARD AND CONCLUDED THAT IT WAS WIND DAMAGE ASSOCIATED WITH A MICROBUST. WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 70-80 MPH. WIDESPREAD SMALL LIMB DAMAGE WAS NOTED AS WELL AS A FEW MEDIUM TO LARGE SIZED TREES DOWNED. A TREE WAS FOUND DOWNED ON A HOME ON PERRIN STREET IN ARABI. THE DAMAGE WAS CONFINED TO AN AREA BOUNDED BY PATRICIA ST TO THE NORTH...ALEXANDER ST TO THE WEST...ROWLEY ST TO THE EAST AND ST. BERNARD HWY. TO THE SOUTH.
